skill.name: biotech-pitch-deck-narrative
skill.description: Structured biotech fundraising narrative design with stage-aware positioning, audience-specific science translation, and explicit assumption tracking for investor presentations.
skill.summary: Build stage-aware biotech pitch narratives that translate complex science into clear investor stories with explicit assumptions and evidence-gap documentation.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated all 65 static analysis findings and determined every one is a false positive. The 40 backtick-execution detections are markdown code fences in SKILL.md documentation, not executable injection. The 12 weak-cryptographic-algorithm detections in scripts/main.py and SKILL.md are triggered by dictionary key names ('key_data') and enum values ('seed') — no cryptographic code exists. The path-traversal detection at SKILL.md:122 is a security checklist item forbidding '../', not an actual traversal. The hardcoded-URL detections are a documentation source attribution link. The env_access detection at scripts/main.py:124 is a false match on 'stage_config.get()'. The two heuristic combination alerts are composites of already-false individual findings. The skill includes a legitimate Python script (scripts/main.py) that uses argparse for safe input handling, writes output to a user-specified file, and contains no network calls, no credential access, and no code execution beyond its documented narrative-generation purpose. No prompt injection attempts were found in any file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 40 backtick patterns in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All occurrences are standard markdown code block fences (```bash) used to document how users run the packaged script. These are documentation, not executable code injection vectors.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dictionary Access as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines containing 'key_data' dictionary access and 'seed' enum value in scripts/main.py as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. These are standard Python dataclass attribute names and PitchStage enum members with no cryptographic relevance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Checklist Item as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged '../' in the SKILL.md security checklist item '- [ ] No unauthorized file system access (../)' as a path traversal sequence. The text is a rule forbidding path traversal, not an attempt to perform one.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Link as Hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the GitHub source attribution URL in SKILL.md line 7 as a hardcoded URL. This is a markdown reference link documenting where the skill source code lives, not a network request made by executable code.

content.user_title: Craft Biotech Investor Pitch Deck Narratives
content.value_statement: Biotech fundraising requires translating complex science into clear investor stories without losing scientific accuracy. This skill helps researchers and founders build structured pitch narratives with stage-aware positioning, audience-specific language, and explicit documentation of assumptions and evidence gaps.
actual_capabilities: Generate stage-aware biotech fundraising narratives for pre-seed through IPO investor presentations
Translate scientific mechanisms into audience-appropriate business value propositions for different investor types
Structure pitch deck narratives with clearly separated facts, assumptions, and risk factors
Support multiple investor audience types including generalist VCs, biotech specialists, and pharma partners
Produce explicit missing-evidence flags and diligence gap documentation for each narrative section
Generate anticipated investor Q&A preparation materials with risk mitigation responses
limitations: Cannot perform live market validation or competitive diligence independently of user-supplied data
Does not provide investment advice, valuation opinions, or regulatory compliance guidance
Requires user-supplied scientific and business evidence as input for narrative generation
Output quality and specificity depend on the completeness of provided inputs

use_cases.title: Seed-Stage Platform Biotech Fundraising
use_cases.description: Create a seed-round pitch narrative that explains a novel therapeutic platform to generalist investors without oversimplifying the underlying science or overstating early-stage evidence.
use_cases.target_user: Biotech startup founders preparing first institutional fundraise

use_cases.title: Science-Heavy Section Rewrite for Investors
use_cases.description: Rewrite dense preclinical or clinical data sections into clear language that non-specialist investors can understand, evaluate, and compare against other opportunities in their portfolio.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researchers transitioning to industry or seeking venture funding

use_cases.title: Risk-Aware Investor Q&A Preparation
use_cases.description: Generate anticipated investor questions and structured responses that acknowledge scientific, regulatory, and market risks while presenting credible mitigation strategies and timeline expectations.
use_cases.target_user: Biotech CEOs and CFOs preparing for fundraising roadshows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pitch Narrative Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a pitch deck narrative for my biotech startup. We are developing a [mechanism] for [disease area]. We are at [stage] stage and targeting [audience] investors. Our key evidence includes: [list available data].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: First-time fundraising preparation with basic company information

prompt_templates.title: Scientific Mechanism Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this scientific mechanism into a business value proposition for [audience] investors. Keep the science accurate but make it accessible to someone without a PhD. Flag any claims that need additional supporting data. Mechanism: [paste description].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Converting technical scientific descriptions into investor-friendly language

prompt_templates.title: Full Deck Narrative with Evidence Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete pitch deck narrative for a [stage] biotech company. Here is what we know: Scientific data: [details], Market data: [details], Team background: [details], Competitive landscape: [details]. For each section, flag any claims that lack supporting evidence and suggest what additional data would strengthen the narrative.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Comprehensive evidence-grounded narrative construction with gap analysis

prompt_templates.title: Section-by-Section Restructuring with Risk Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite each section of my pitch deck for a [audience] investor audience. For every section provide: (1) the rewritten narrative, (2) assumptions made during translation, (3) missing evidence flags, (4) potential investor concerns likely to arise, and (5) suggested risk mitigation language. Sections to rewrite: [paste each section with its title and current content].
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: Complete narrative restructuring with multi-layer analysis per section

output_examples.input: We have a novel mRNA delivery platform for solid tumors at seed stage targeting generalist VCs. We have preclinical mouse data showing 60% tumor reduction.
output_examples.output:
HOOK: Addressing the critical unmet need in solid tumor treatment through a novel mRNA delivery platform — a first-in-class approach that overcomes the key delivery barriers that have limited previous RNA therapeutics.

PROBLEM: Solid tumors remain the leading cause of cancer mortality worldwide. Current treatments cannot target tumor cells selectively without severe systemic toxicity, leaving patients with limited options and poor quality of life during treatment.

SOLUTION: Our platform achieves targeted intracellular delivery of mRNA payloads to solid tumor cells, enabling precise therapeutic intervention while sparing healthy tissue.

EVIDENCE STATUS: Preclinical mouse models demonstrate 60% tumor reduction. [Assumption: Results are from controlled studies with appropriate sample sizes. Missing: Large-animal toxicity data, manufacturing scalability assessment.]

output_examples.input: Rewrite our mechanism of action slide for generalist VCs. Our technology uses CRISPR-Cas12 for point-of-care infectious disease diagnostics. Published in Nature Biotechnology 2025.
output_examples.output:
OUR TECHNOLOGY: We built a diagnostic platform that works like a precise molecular search-and-identify system. It finds disease-specific genetic signatures in patient samples and produces a clear result within 30 minutes — no lab equipment or specialized training needed. Think of it as bringing the accuracy of a central lab test into any clinic, pharmacy, or community health setting.

SCIENTIFIC BASIS: CRISPR-Cas12 collateral cleavage activity enables amplified signal detection from attomolar-level target concentrations, achieving lab-grade sensitivity in a portable format.

EVIDENCE STATUS: Published in Nature Biotechnology (2025). [Assumption: Publication demonstrates analytical validity for claimed target pathogens. Missing: Prospective clinical validation data, manufacturing cost analysis at scale, regulatory pathway determination.]

best_practices: Provide complete scientific context including mechanism, disease area, development stage, and all available evidence before requesting a full narrative
Review and validate every factual claim in the generated narrative against your actual data before presenting to any investor
Use the explicit assumptions and missing-evidence flags to prioritize your follow-up data collection and due diligence preparation
anti_patterns: Do not present generated narratives as final investor materials without thorough human review and evidence verification for every claim
Do not use the skill to invent or fabricate scientific data, clinical trial results, or market statistics that you do not possess
Do not treat the generated risk assessments or Q&A responses as substitutes for legal counsel or regulatory due diligence

faq.question: What types of biotech companies can use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works for therapeutic, diagnostic, platform, and medical device companies at any funding stage from pre-seed through IPO. It adapts narrative depth and focus based on the stage and audience you specify.

faq.question: Does this skill require programming knowledge?
faq.answer: No programming knowledge is required. You provide your scientific and business context in plain language, and the skill generates structured investor-facing narrative text. The packaged Python script is optional for additional processing.

faq.question: Can this skill create financial projections or valuation models?
faq.answer: No. The skill structures narrative messaging around market opportunity and business potential but does not generate financial models, specific valuation figures, or revenue projections. Those require separate financial modeling tools.

faq.question: What inputs does the skill need to work effectively?
faq.answer: The skill works best when you provide your disease area, mechanism of action, development stage, target investor audience, available preclinical or clinical evidence, and known competitive landscape. More complete inputs produce more specific narratives.

faq.question: Is the generated content ready to show investors?
faq.answer: The output is a narrative scaffold that requires your careful review. You must verify all claims against your actual data, add your specific evidence, and ensure regulatory compliance before presenting to any investor audience.

faq.question: How is this different from asking Claude to write a pitch deck directly?
faq.answer: This skill adds structured stage-aware framing, audience-specific scientific translation, explicit assumption tracking, evidence-gap documentation, and risk-factor separation that a general-purpose prompt does not systematically include.
